entry.notes: [dahj] is a loan from nepali, but there is no indication that ['amɔ] is.
used for "affirmative, absolute classification" according to sno1 and "identification" according to sno2

entry.nb: quot meaning obscure "cause to not even think of sthg", verbatim "disappear"

entry.notes: gloss reads 'be bent' in sno1 and
entry given by mm, not in sno2;
entry.nb: entries in this section off by one number in hari and in hale; this could possibly be 'cloth' *kwan, and not cotton *kammm, 91

entry.notes: gloss and entry were skipped in hale (see swadesh 100 word list)

entry.nb: the stencil mark for the letter 'o' in 'koh is blurred. i believe it should be 'e' whence ⁴ke 'work'. the mistake was propagated in hale et al.mm, sept 91

entry.notes: same entry under 1.63, except for suffix (see previous card)

entry.nb: numbering in this section of hari and of hale is off by one

entry.hw: ³kur˥mu ³kur ³kur ¹lə =ləmu kuhr kuhr 'lɔ-lɔ
²kʰasjək³nə =ri ²kʰasjək ²kʰusjuk ¹lə=lənɔh-ri kʰasjɔk kʰusjuk 'lɔ-lɔ

entry.nb: "to remain afloat", glossed as "to float" in sil
'to stick a flower in the hair'mm, 9.91

entry.hw: ²kʰusjuk³nə =ri ²kʰasjək ²kʰusjuk ¹lə=lənɔh-ri kʰasjɔk kʰusjuk 'lɔ-lɔ
²laka⁴ke ²laka ²loko ¹lələ'keh laka loko 'lɔlɔ
²loko⁴ke ²laka ²loko ¹lələ'keh laka loko 'lɔlɔ

entry.notes: = 'churn buttered tea' according to both sources
as in "la flamme br̂ʊle = le feu marche" (mm)
= 'break things into small pieces by wringing'
same entry under 1.57, except for suffix
used for "existence, presupposition" according to sno1 and "existence" according to sno2
entered as ['nahngya] and glossed as 'bedstead' in hale (6.a.33, p.146)
entered as ['nɔhnŋja] and glossed as 'bed' in sno2

entry.nb: second half of compound = non distinctive tone

entry.hw: ³nuŋ =nə³naŋ ³naŋ ⁴ke ³nuŋ=nə ¹lə=lənahŋ nahŋ 'keh nuhŋ-nɔ 'lɔ-lɔ

entry.notes: also glossed as 'ask insistingly' in sn02

entry.emp: nep bhal; "water like bhal will come"

entry.notes: not used for fruit, strip skin off animals, and stripping of skin through wounding
'tidy up a dry field after sowing' in sn02.
same entry under 1.65 (previous card), except for suffix
entered as [tɔ pʰe pucj] in sno1
[raŋ] is a loan from nepali (see 12.b.37 and 19.19), but i don't know if [pʰopʈe] is too.
[raŋ] is a loan from nepali (see 12.b.37 and 19.19), but i don't know if [pʰopʈe] is too.
= pull out a knife from its sheath

entry.nb: ³nəsə means 'sickness', whence ?? healthy, cf ³nəsə ⁴jə-tsi-wə

entry.notes: same entry under 18.b.27, except for different suffix
used for "happening" according to both sources

entry.nb: ? emp from nep tulana: garnumm, sept 91. this is the only /tr/ in the whole lexicon.

entry.notes: [tʰara] is a loan from nepali meaning 'sterile' (mm)

entry.cf: ¹tʰo=wə everywhere else, including ⁴tʰaksaŋ ¹tʰo=wə (halep.279)

entry.notes: also glossed as 'thick wool blanket' in sno1

entry.nb: tonal inconsistency between 20.15 and 19.3 confirmed by mm,91
tonal inconsistency between 20.15 and 19.3 confirmed by mm,91

entry.notes: entered as [bɔ-lɔ] in sno1, which might be a typo.

entry.hw: ³tsilik˥mu ³tsilik ³tsilik ¹lə =ləmu cihlik cihlik 'lɔ-lɔ

entry.notes: contruction = v-wɔ + cja-lɔ (mm); same entry under 'start'
contruction = v-wɔ + cja-lɔ (mm); same entry under 'be about (doing sthg)'

entry.nb: half-moon is ²tsʰəjwə ⁴pre or ²tsʰəiwə ¹tso-pre, ? =8 and 18?
